questions.question: Define an Operating System and explain its role as an intermediary between users and hardware.
keyPoints: OS is system software managing hardware resources
Acts as intermediary between user and hardware
Provides convenient environment for program execution
Allocates resources efficiently

questions.question: What are the three main goals of an Operating System? Explain each briefly.
keyPoints: Execute user programs - makes solving problems easier
Make computer convenient - user-friendly interface
Use hardware efficiently - optimal resource utilization

questions.question: Explain the concept of multiprogramming and its advantages.
keyPoints: Multiple programs in memory simultaneously
CPU switches between programs when one waits for I/O
Increases CPU utilization and throughput
Reduces idle time of CPU

questions.question: What is the kernel of an Operating System? What are its main functions?
keyPoints: Kernel is the core component running in privileged mode
Manages CPU scheduling and process management
Controls memory allocation and management
Handles file system operations and device drivers

questions.question: List and briefly explain five main functions of an Operating System.
keyPoints: Process Management - creating, scheduling, terminating processes
Memory Management - allocating and deallocating memory
File Management - organizing and accessing files
Device Management - controlling I/O devices
Protection and Security - controlling access to resources

questions.question: What are system calls? Give examples of different types of system calls.
keyPoints: System calls provide interface between user programs and OS
Process control - create, terminate, load, execute programs
File management - open, close, read, write files
Device management - request, release devices
Information maintenance - get/set time, date

questions.question: Explain the layered architecture of an Operating System with its advantages.
keyPoints: OS designed in layers from hardware to user interface
Each layer uses services of lower layers
Provides modularity and ease of debugging
Simplifies system design and maintenance
Examples: Windows NT, UNIX with layers

questions.question: Discuss in detail the various services provided by an Operating System to both users and processes. Explain how these services make computing convenient.
keyPoints: Program execution - loading and running programs
I/O operations - performing I/O on behalf of user processes
File system manipulation - creating, deleting, reading, writing files
Communications - exchange of data between processes
Error detection - handling errors in CPU, memory, I/O devices
Resource allocation - distributing CPU time, memory, storage to processes
Accounting - tracking resource usage statistics
Protection - ensuring controlled access to system resources

questions.question: Explain the different types of Operating Systems based on their usage and architecture. Give examples of each.
keyPoints: Batch Processing OS - processes jobs in batches, example: early IBM systems
Time-sharing OS - multiple users share CPU, example: UNIX, Multics
Real-time OS - immediate response for critical systems, example: RTOS
Distributed OS - multiple computers appear as single system, example: AIX
Network OS - provides network services, example: Windows Server
Mobile OS - for handheld devices, example: Android, iOS
Embedded OS - for specific hardware, example: embedded Linux

questions.question: What is the difference between system software and application software? Also explain how the Operating System functions as system software.
keyPoints: System software manages hardware (OS, compilers, device drivers)
Application software performs user tasks (games, word processors)
OS as system software provides: resource management, hardware abstraction, platform for applications
OS loads before other software and remains in memory
Controls all hardware access through device drivers
Provides common services used by all applications

questions.question: Describe the various computing environments in which Operating Systems operate. How has the role of OS evolved with changing computing environments?
keyPoints: Traditional computing - standalone PCs and workstations
Client-server computing - network with central servers
Distributed computing - multiple computers working together
Peer-to-peer computing - equal peers sharing resources
Cloud computing - virtualized resources over internet
Mobile computing - smartphones and tablets
Internet of Things (IoT) - embedded systems
Evolution: From simple batch systems to complex multi-purpose systems, support for networking, virtualization, cloud integration, and mobile platforms
